manifest.description: Adobe OakPAL rules for AEM content package validation (35 rules covering indexing, structure, compatibility)
manifest.purpose: Validate JCR content package structure, Oak indexes, and Cloud Service compatibility

rules.rationale: [Adobe CQBP-84] Don't implement/extend @ProviderType APIs — backward-compatibility risk across versions
[Adobe] Never create/modify nodes under /libs — read-only; upgrade risks
[Adobe] Custom Lucene indexes must have Tika config node for text extraction
[Adobe] Lucene indexes require 'async: [async]' property for asynchronous indexing
[Adobe] DAM asset indexes must include tags property with 'visualSimilaritySearch'
[Adobe] Only Lucene type allowed for custom indexes; legacy types must migrate before Cloud Service
[Adobe] Don't specify queryPaths in damAssetLucene — use includedPaths only
[Adobe] Set compatVersion: 2 for all custom Lucene indexes for forward compatibility
[Adobe] Index child nodes must be nt:unstructured to allow flexible indexing rules
[Adobe] Lucene index must contain indexRules node with at least 1 child rule
[Adobe] Remove deprecated 'seed' property from index definitions
[Adobe] Remove deprecated 'reindex' property from index definitions
[Adobe] Custom indexes must be direct children of /oak:index (not nested)
[Adobe] Ensure includedPaths and queryPaths consistency in index definitions
[Adobe] Don't configure the same OSGi component twice per runmode
[Adobe] /config/ and /install/ folders should only contain OSGi configuration nodes
[Adobe] Multiple packages shouldn't write to the same JCR node paths
[Adobe] Separate /apps and /libs (immutable) from /etc (mutable) into different content packages
[Adobe] No duplicate property names in index definitions
[Adobe] Don't default to deprecated Classic UI authoring (v6.4+); provide Touch UI
[Adobe] Provide Touch UI dialogs alongside Classic UI dialogs for all components
[Adobe] Reverse replication agents not supported in Cloud Service
[Adobe] Update legacy workflow processes for asset micro-services (replace DAM Update Asset Workflow)
[Adobe] Follow strict Cloud Service runmode naming policy (author, publish, preview, etc.)
[Adobe] Use editable templates instead of static templates (better AEM authoring)
[Adobe] Migrate Foundation Components to Core Components (actively maintained)
[Adobe] Place static resources in 'resources' child folder for proper proxy handling
[Adobe] Don't override queryLimitReads in indexes (causes slow reads at scale)
[Adobe] No spaces in indexing definition property names
[Adobe] Cannot modify system indexes: nodetypeLucene, slingResourceResolver, socialLucene, appsLibsLucene, authorizables, pathReference
[Adobe] Deploy custom indexes in separate package from UI content (better versioning)
[Adobe] Custom asset indexes must be prefixed with 'damAssetLucene' for consistency
[Adobe] Analyzer tokenizers must be named 'tokenizer' in Lucene analyzer config

invocation.description: OakPAL validation is typically run via Maven plugin or Cloud Manager. To validate locally:

notes: OakPAL rules validate JCR content package structure and Oak index configurations
These are distinct from Java/backend rules (PMD/Checkstyle) which validate code
Cloud Manager enforces these rules automatically during cloud builds
Use locally during development to catch issues before pushing
Some rules are blockers (must be fixed), others are warnings
